warning.label: Important:
warning.text: Swimming is currently prohibited in the Neckar (Stuttgart municipality)!

items.title: Swimming guidelines
items.text: On federal waterways such as the Neckar, it is prohibited to swim in shipping lanes or within 100 metres of structures such as weirs, locks and bridges. Anyone who wants to swim in rivers safely should also follow a few rules.

items.title: Pollution
items.text: Following heavy rainfall, the river may temporarily become polluted with microorganisms. For healthy people, the risk of serious illness is low. However, people with pre-existing medical conditions should be especially cautious.

items.title: Large Vessels
items.text: Ships up to 105 metres long travel on the Neckar. They cannot change course, which creates dangerous wake effects. Make sure you stay well away from the shipping channel in the middle and any shipping infrastructure.

items.title: Local Conditions
items.text: Although the Neckar is not a fast-flowing river, dangerous currents can form near bridges and other structures, or as a result of passing ships. Poor visibility makes it harder to spot hazards beneath the water's surface. Anyone swimming in the Neckar should be familiar with the local conditions.
